fields.timestamp_end: ISO8601 UTC — when service restored (null if ongoing)
fields.resolution: free text — how it was fixed
fields.mttr_minutes: integer — mean time to recover (same as duration for single incidents)
fields.recurrence: boolean — has this type occurred before?
fields.notion_page_id: string — linked Notion page ID (null if not synced)

incidents.trigger: Planned circuit shutdown — power trip on OC1 host circuit
incidents.rca: Deliberate power circuit shutdown (planned maintenance). OC1 (Mac mini) shut down ungracefully. On restart, FileVault full-disk encryption required manual login before OpenClaw gateway and agents could resume. No data loss. No config corruption detected.
incidents.resolution: Manual physical login at OC1 console to unlock FileVault. Gateway and all agents resumed automatically post-login.

incidents.trigger: openclaw update command triggered SIGKILL — gateway self-restarted, session context lost
incidents.rca: Platform update (openclaw update 2026.4.24) triggered self-restart via SIGKILL. Session context not flushed beforehand. Ken required double memory recall to restore Sprint 2 context. Root cause: no pre-risky-op checkpoint protocol existed.
incidents.resolution: Session restored via session file forensics. Pre-risky-op checkpoint rule created. Memory search doctor error fixed (OLLAMA_HOST + remote config). Gateway restarted clean.
incidents.prevention: Pre-risky-op checkpoint rule (NON-NEGOTIABLE) — flush all context before any OpenClaw update/restart
